title: git-std configuration
description: Schema for .git-std.toml — the configuration file for git-std, a single CLI that replaces commitizen, commitlint, standard-version, husky, and lefthook.

$schema.description: JSON Schema URL for editor validation and autocomplete.

scheme.description: Versioning scheme. semver: conventional bump rules (breaking → major, feat → minor, else → patch). calver: date-based versioning using calver_format. patch: always increments patch only, rejects breaking changes unless --force.

types.description: Allowed conventional commit types. Used for validation in strict mode and for the interactive commit prompt.

oneOf.description: Auto-discover scopes from workspace layout (crates/*, packages/*, modules/*).

scopes.description: Scope validation. Omit for no validation, set to "auto" for workspace discovery, or provide an explicit list.

strict.description: Enforce types and scopes validation without requiring the --strict CLI flag.

calver_format.description: Calendar version format. Tokens: YYYY (full year), YY (short year), 0M (zero-padded month), MM (month), WW (ISO week), DD (day), PATCH (auto-incrementing counter), DP (day-of-week + patch). Only used when scheme = "calver".

title.description: Custom changelog title heading. If absent, no title is added.

hidden.description: Commit types excluded from the changelog.

bug_url.description: Base URL for bug/issue links in changelog entries (e.g. https://github.com/org/repo/issues).

sections.description: Map commit types to changelog section headings. Unlisted types use the type name as heading.

version_files.description: Custom version files to update during bump, in addition to auto-detected files.

path.description: File path relative to the repository root.

regex.description: Regex whose first capture group contains the version string.
